definition: The ratio of the current pot size to the cost of a contemplated call. Used to determine whether a call is profitable given your equity in the hand.
example: Pot is $100, villain bets $50. You must call $50 to win $150, giving you 3:1 pot odds (25% equity needed).
usage: Use pot odds to decide borderline calls on the flop and turn when you have a drawing hand.
